result.stderr:
nanoff 2.5.131+6bd4d0daab
Copyright (c) .NET Foundation and Contributors
USAGE:
- Update ESP32 WROVER Kit device with latest available firmware:
  nanoff --target ESP_WROVER_KIT --update
- Update specific STM32 device (ST_STM32F769I_DISCOVERY) with latest available
firmware, using JTAG interface:
  nanoff --jtag --target ST_STM32F769I_DISCOVERY --update
- Update ESP32 device with latest available firmware (stable version), device is
connected to COM31:
  nanoff --platform esp32 --serialport COM31 --update
- Update specific ESP32 device with custom firmware (local bin file):
  nanoff --image "<location of file>.bin" --target ESP_WROVER_KIT
- Update specific Silabs device (Giant Gecko EVK) with latest available
firmware:
  nanoff --target SL_STK3701A --update
- List all STM32 devices connected through JTAG:
  nanoff --listjtag --platform stm32
- Install STM32 JTAG drivers:
  nanoff --installjtagdrivers
- List all available STM32 targets:
  nanoff --listtargets --platform stm32
- List all available COM ports:
  nanoff --listports

  --listdfu                       (Default: false) List connected DFU devices.

  --dfuid                         ID of the DFU device to update. If not
                                  specified the first connected DFU device will
                                  be used.

  --dfu                           (Default: false) Use DFU to update the device.

  --installdfudrivers             (Default: false) Install STM32 DFU drivers.

  --jtagid                        ID of the JTAG device to update. If not
                                  specified the first connected JTAG device will
                                  be used.

  --listjtag                      (Default: false) List connected STM32 JTAG
                                  devices.

  --hexfile                       HEX file(s) to be flashed into the device.
                                  Only JTAG connected targets are supported.

  --binfile                       BIN file(s) to be flashed into the device.

  --jtag                          (Default: false) Use JTAG to update the
                                  device.

  --installjtagdrivers            (Default: false) Install STM32 JTAG drivers.

  --serialport                    Serial port where device is connected to.

  --baud                          (Default: 1500000) Baud rate to use for the
                                  serial port.

  --flashmode                     (Default: dio) Flash mode to use.

  --flashfreq                     (Default: 40) Flash frequency to use [MHz].

  --partitiontablesize            Partition table size to use. Valid sizes are:
                                  2, 4, 8 and 16.

  --checkpsram                    (Default: false) Perform check for PSRAM in
                                  device.

  --nobackupconfig                (Default: false) Skip backup of configuration
                                  partition.

  --installxdsdrivers             (Default: false) Install XDS110 drivers.

  --listjlink                     (Default: false) List connected USB J-Link
                                  devices.

  --jlinkid                       ID of the J-Link device to update. If not
                                  specified the first connected J-Link device
                                  will be used.

  --setvcpbr                      Set baud rate of J-Link Virtual COM Port. If a
                                  value is not specified it will use the default
                                  value for Wire Protocol.

  --nanodevice                    (Default: false) Operations are to be
                                  performed to a nanoFramework device.

  --clrfile                       Path to file with CLR image.

  --target                        Target name. This is the target name used in
                                  the GitHub and Cloudsmith repositories.

  --platform                      Target platform. Acceptable values are: esp32,
                                  stm32, cc13x2, efm32.

  -v, --verbosity                 (Default: n) Sets the verbosity level of the
                                  command. Allowed values are q[uiet],
                                  m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and
                                  diag[nostic]. Not supported in every command;
                                  see specific command page to determine if this
                                  option is available.

  --backuppath                    Path where to store the backup file.

  --backupfile                    Backup file name. Just the file name without
                                  path. If the file name isn't provided one will
                                  be generated.

  --fwversion                     Firmware version to flash the device with.

  --update                        (Default: false) Update the device firmware
                                  using the other specified options.

  --deploy                        (Default: false) Flash a deployment image
                                  specified with --image.

  --preview                       (Default: false) Download a firmware package
                                  from the preview repository that includes
                                  major changes or experimental features.

  --image                         Path to deployment image file to be uploaded
                                  to device.

  --masserase                     (Default: false) Mass erase the device flash
                                  before uploading the firmware. If more than
                                  one file is specified to be flashed the mass
                                  erase will be performed before the first file
                                  is flashed.

  --address                       Address(es) where to flash the BIN file(s).
                                  Hexadecimal format (e.g. 0x08000000). Required
                                  when specifying a BIN file with --binfile
                                  argument or flashing a deployment image with
                                  --deploy argument.

  --reset                         (Default: false) Perform reset on connected
                                  device after all other requested operations
                                  are successfully performed.

  --nofitcheck                    (Default: false) Skip execution of sanity
                                  check if the requested target fits the
                                  connected device. This is a best effort
                                  validation and it's NOT guaranted to be fail
                                  safe.

  --listtargets                   (Default: false) List the available targets
                                  and versions. --platform and --preview options
                                  apply.

  --listports                     (Default: false) List the all the COM ports on
                                  this machine.

  --clearcache                    (Default: false) Clear the cache folder with
                                  firmware images.

  --listdevices                   (Default: false) List the .NET nanoFramework
                                  devices connected to the machine.

  --devicedetails                 (Default: false) Reads details from connected
                                  device.

  --identifyfirmware              (Default: false) Show which firmware to use
                                  for a device without deploying anything.

  --filedeployment                JSON file containing file deployment settings.

  --networkdeployment             JSON file containing network deployment
                                  settings.

  --archivepath                   Path of the directory where the firmware is
                                  archived.

  --updatearchive                 (Default: false) Copy the firmware from the
                                  online repository to the firmware archive
                                  directory; do not update the firmware on a
                                  connected device.

  --fromarchive                   (Default: false) Get the firmware from the
                                  firmware archive rather than from the online
                                  repository.

  --suppressnanoffversioncheck    (Default: false) Do not check whether a new
                                  version of nanoff is available.

  --help                          Display this help screen.

  --version                       Display version information.
